title: Encryption-at-rest coverage gap — unencrypted storage with regulated data
exclusions: Does NOT judge algorithm strength (see UC-22.41.2); focuses on coverage only.
evidence: Daily report 'encryption_at_rest_gap' writes unencrypted-volume findings to compliance_summary.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Art.32(1)(a) encryption is a recommended technical measure; gap detection is a leading indicator of Art.32 compliance.
§164.312(a)(2)(iv) encryption addressable specification — the UC provides evidence of coverage.
PCI 3.5 requires rendering stored account data unreadable; the UC detects any CHD-scoped storage without encryption.
SC-13 cryptographic protection — the UC evidences coverage for regulated data.
Derived from GDPR@2016/679 Art.32 (parent assurance='partial'). Clause mapped per data/regulations.json derivesFrom graph; inherited assurance degraded one step.
Derived from GDPR@2016/679 Art.32 (parent assurance='partial'). Clause mapped per data/regulations.json derivesFrom graph; inherited assurance degraded one step.
Derived from GDPR@2016/679 Art.32 (parent assurance='partial'). UK GDPR preserves parent clause numbering; inherited assurance degraded one step.

controlTest.positiveScenario: When cloud-storage or database volumes tagged regulated_data=true are observed without an active encryption configuration (EBS-encryption, SSE-KMS, TDE), the UC fires naming the volume and classification.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Volumes with an encryption key ARN and AES-256 / AES-GCM active do NOT fire.

spl:
| rest /services/cloud_storage/inventory splunk_server=* | where regulated_data="true"
| stats values(encryption_state) as state by volume_id region tenant
| where mvfind(state, "enabled")<0
| lookup data_classification.csv volume_id OUTPUT classification
| table tenant region volume_id classification state
description: Produces a daily inventory of storage volumes that hold regulated data but are not encrypted at rest. Findings feed the remediation backlog and the evidence pack.
value: Unencrypted regulated data is the most common PCI 3.5 / HIPAA finding — preventing it from appearing on the audit spreadsheet is the goal.
implementation: (1) Ingest cloud-inventory telemetry; (2) tag volumes via data_classification.csv; (3) run daily; (4) on finding, auto-open a CISO review.
visualization: Stacked bar of encrypted vs unencrypted by tenant, table of findings, single-value '% of regulated volumes encrypted'.
knownFalsePositives: Volumes undergoing pre-encryption migration require a time-bounded allowance (migration_end_date).
